meta.reportLabel: Sat weekend wrap — Friday's close UNDID Thursday's soft-USD move: DXY re-firmed to ~101, EUR/USD sank to a fresh 1-yr low ~1.1415, USD/JPY drifted to ~162.5 (near 40-yr lows, MoF verbal-only). Board less RANGE - USD the firm leg again, JPY the underperformer; short-EUR/USD back on (MED). Commodities split: WTI ~$71.2 but +3.5% wk on the Hormuz premium (US strikes Jul 7-9), silver snapped back to ~$60.4, gold held ~$4,122 (-1.5% wk). Jul 14 US CPI is the single arbiter.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: Friday's close reversed Thursday's soft-USD dip: the dollar re-firmed (DXY back to ~101), EUR/USD sank to a fresh one-year low (~1.1415), and USD/JPY drifted up to ~162.5 near 40-year lows with the MoF still on verbal-only watch. The board is less RANGE than Thursday looked - the USD is the firm leg again, the yen the clear underperformer, and short-EUR/USD is back on as a medium call. Commodities split: renewed US strikes (Jul 7-9, ~90 targets) put a Hormuz risk premium back, so WTI closed ~$71.2 but +3.5% on the week, silver snapped back to ~$60.4 and gold held ~$4,122. The fragile de-escalation is the two-way wildcard, but Jul 14 US CPI is the single arbiter of whether the firm-USD close extends or breaks.
meta.nextBigEvent: Tue Jul 14, 12:30 UTC: US June CPI - oil base softens the headline but core is seen firm (~2.9%, +0.3% m/m); decides the Sept hike (~45-50%) and whether the re-firmed USD extends or breaks.

dailyRead: Friday's close undid Thursday's soft-USD move: the dollar re-firmed, the yen slid, and oil booked a weekly gain on the Hormuz premium. DXY closed the week back at ~101 (up from Thursday's ~100.9) as the higher-for-longer story reasserted into Jul 14 CPI, EUR/USD sank to ~1.1415 - a fresh one-year low - and USD/JPY drifted up to ~162.5, back near 40-year lows with the MoF still on verbal-only watch (Y11.73tn/$73bn April-May precedent unused). The macro board is therefore LESS RANGE than Thursday looked: the USD is the firm leg again, the yen is the clear underperformer, and short-EUR/USD is back on as a medium call. Commodities split - WTI closed ~$71.2 but +3.5% on the week as renewed US strikes (Jul 7-9, ~90 targets after Iran hit three vessels in Hormuz) put a risk premium back, silver snapped back to ~$60.4 (from ~$57.7) and gold held ~$4,122 (-1.5% wk). The fragile de-escalation - mediators reconvening, Trump saying Iran 'reached out,' Iran's team 'distrustful' - is the two-way wildcard, but Jul 14 US CPI is the single arbiter: it decides whether the firm-USD close extends or breaks. Stand down size into it.

today.headline: Spot drifts to ~162.5, back near 40-yr lows as yields firm
today.read: The yield fade reversed and the carry grind resumed - the JPY case is now purely the MoF backstop-asymmetry near 163 (Y11.73tn precedent unused, verbal-only). BoJ Jul 31 stays hawkish. A push to 163+ forces the MoF's hand; a hot CPI extends the grind.

today.headline: Bid RE-FIRMED - DXY back to ~101 into CPI
today.read: Thursday's soft-USD dip healed by Friday's close as higher-for-longer reasserted; SEP still 1-2 hikes, zero cuts, Sept ~45-50%. Jul 14 CPI is the only reset - core seen firm (+0.3% m/m, ~2.9%) even as the oil base softens the headline. A soft headline misread dovish cracks it; a hot core snaps it higher.

today.headline: EUR/USD sinks to a fresh 1-year low ~1.1415
today.read: The euro made a new one-year low as the USD re-firmed; oil-driven inflation is lifting Sept ECB-hike bets but that stays a forward story, not a spot bid. Short-EUR/USD is re-armed (MED). A hot US CPI extends the slide; a hawkish Jul 23 core tone squeezes shorts.

today.headline: Jobs beat + weekly oil gain firm the CAD floor
today.read: Friday's +18.2k beat plus WTI +3.5% on the week held USD/CAD off its ~1.42 highs, but the internals are hollow (part-time-only, mfg -17k) and the BoC stays sidelined Jul 15. A hot US CPI re-fires USD/CAD; an oil slide + soft-internals re-read re-softens CAD.

today.headline: Gold holds ~$4,122 (-1.5% wk); silver snaps back to ~$60.4
today.read: A firmer USD and steadier yields capped gold while the Hormuz haven bid underpinned; silver's sharp recovery (from ~$57.7) outpaced it on the industrial-plus-haven combo. Range into CPI - a hot print re-arms the real-rate selloff; a Hormuz shock with a haven bid revives the long.

today.headline: No change - mixed; firm USD caps the beta
today.read: The sticky-core floor stands but the Aug 11 hike tail is thin (~36%), soft China CPI (+1%) trims the pulse, and a firmer USD caps AUD/USD. Q2 CPI late Jul is the re-arm. A Hormuz re-escalation flips it risk-off.

today.headline: No change - dovish-hike NZD, higher hurdle for the next move
today.read: The 6-0 hike to 2.50% was scripted dovish (inflation seen peaked 3.9% Q2); NZ Q2 CPI ~Jul 16 is the swing factor. A firm USD leads NZD/USD, dovish NZD caps the bounce. A 4%+ CPI re-arms the hawks.

today.headline: No change - firm and quiet; a firm USD caps GBP/USD
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, and GBP/USD holds >1.34 but the re-firmed dollar caps it. UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the check; a soft UK data wave re-splits the MPC.

strength.tag: DXY back to ~101; higher-for-longer reasserts into CPI
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss (unemp 4.2%); Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, SEP pencils 1-2 hikes and zero 2026 cuts, Sept ~45-50%. Thursday's soft-USD dip healed by Friday's close - DXY back to ~101 as higher-for-longer reasserted into CPI.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Firm into the Jul 28-29 FOMC; SEP caps the downside and Jul 14 CPI is the only trigger that resets direction - core seen firm (+0.3% m/m, ~2.9%) even as the oil base softens the headline. Risk: a soft/negative headline misread dovish cracks the bid; a hot core snaps it higher. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.tag: services CPI 3.7%; firm USD caps GBP/USD
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); GBP/USD holds >1.34 but a re-firmed USD caps it.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round. Risk: a soft UK activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC; UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the check. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: 2.50%, first hike since 2023; higher hurdle next
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0 - but the statement was the dovish version: headline inflation now seen PEAKED at 3.9% in Q2 (easing to 3.3% Q3), a 'considerably higher hurdle' for the next move. Soft China CPI (+1%) trims the growth story; a firm USD leads NZD/USD.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hike delivered but guidance capped it - Sept still likely, not aggressively priced; Aug 19 should be a hold. NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) validates or breaks the 'peaked at 3.9%' framing. Risk: a 4%+ CPI print re-arms the hawks; risk-off beta cuts the other way. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.tag: jobs beat + oil +3.5% wk hold USD/CAD off ~1.42
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k BEAT ~+10k, unemp fell to 6.5% - but all part-time, goods-producing -43.7k, manufacturing -17k; BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold; technical recession. The jobs beat + a weekly oil gain firmed the CAD floor.
strength.forward: ⚖️ The Friday jobs beat + WTI +3.5% on the week firmed the floor and held USD/CAD off its highs, but the internals are hollow and the BoC stays sidelined Jul 15. Risk: a hot US CPI re-fires USD/CAD; an oil slide + soft internals re-soften CAD. Next BoC Jul 15.

strength.tag: thin Aug tail (~36%), China CPI +1%, firm USD caps
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening (June jobs Jul 16). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); the Aug 11 hike is priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%, trimming the demand pulse; a firmer USD caps AUD/USD.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 The sticky-core floor stands but the thin hike tail + soft China + a firm USD cap it. Risk: a Hormuz re-escalation flips risk-off; a durable de-escalation plus a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) flips it hawkish. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting; EUR/USD sank to a fresh one-year low (~1.1415) as the USD re-firmed.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected) while oil-driven inflation lifts Sept-hike bets - but that stays a forward story, not a spot bid. Risk: a hot US CPI extends the slide; a hawkish Jul 23 core tone hands EUR its own leg and squeezes shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: spot ~162.5; yields firm, carry grind resumes, MoF verbal-only
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. Spot drifted back to ~162.5 (near 40-year lows) as US yields firmed into CPI - the carry grind resumed and the yield fade reversed. MoF verbal-only, Y11.73tn precedent unused.
strength.forward: 🦅 The JPY case is now purely the backstop-asymmetry near 163; BoJ Jul 31 stays hawkish. Risk: a push to 163+ forces the MoF's hand; a hot CPI extends the carry grind. Next BoJ Jul 31.

symbols.why: Re-armed: EUR closed at a fresh 1-yr low (~1.1415) as the USD re-firmed (DXY ~101); core-2.9% keeps the US floor into CPI while EUR's hike bets stay a forward story, not a spot bid.
symbols.risk: A soft/negative headline CPI misread dovish reverses it; a hawkish ECB Jul 23 core tone squeezes EUR shorts.

symbols.why: Friday jobs beat + a weekly oil gain firmed CAD off ~1.42, but a re-firmed USD offsets - two-way into CPI/BoC, no clean vector.
symbols.risk: A hot US CPI re-fires the long; an oil slide + soft-internals re-read weakens CAD again.

symbols.why: Spot ground back to ~162.5 as yields firmed - the carry grind resumed; the only cap is the MoF backstop-asymmetry near 163. Long-carry blocked by intervention risk.
symbols.risk: A break above 163 triggers the MoF and snaps it down; a hot CPI extends the grind higher.

symbols.why: Closed ~$71.2 but +3.5% on the week as renewed strikes (Jul 7-9) restored a Hormuz risk premium; the live tail keeps a thin long bias despite the glut cap.
symbols.risk: A Doha breakthrough crushes it; a tanker/Hormuz-closure shock re-arms it.

symbols.why: Soft EUR (1-yr low) vs an NZD that just hiked; the dovish statement caps the NZD leg but the EUR weakness leads.
symbols.risk: A hawkish NZ Q2 CPI extends it; an EUR core squeeze reverses it.

symbols.why: RBA drifting to hold vs an RBNZ that actually delivered - both legs softened, the edge is thin.
symbols.risk: A hot AU Q2 CPI flips it; NZD's higher-hurdle guidance caps the divergence.

symbols.why: Yen the weaker leg (162.5) gives a mild carry-long tilt, but a firm USD + thin AU tail cap AUD - momentum thin.
symbols.risk: A Hormuz re-escalation flips risk-off; a MoF strike snaps it down.

symbols.why: Two weak legs - soft EUR vs a yield-driven-weaker JPY with the MoF backstop; no clean vector.
symbols.risk: A hawkish ECB Jul 23; a MoF strike lifts JPY.

symbols.why: Dovish-hike NZD vs a weaker JPY - mild carry tilt, both legs soft.
symbols.risk: A hot NZ Q2 CPI re-arms NZD; a MoF strike lifts JPY.

symbols.why: A firmer USD + thin hike tail + soft China cap it; the sticky-core floor holds it two-way.
symbols.risk: A Hormuz shock + a hot US CPI presses it down; a durable de-escalation + hot Q2 CPI lifts it.

symbols.why: CAD firmed on jobs + oil while JPY weakened on yields - mild long tilt, capped by intervention risk near 163.
symbols.risk: A MoF strike lifts JPY; an oil slide softens CAD.

symbols.why: CAD firmer on jobs + oil while AUD stays mixed - both legs soft, the edge is thin.
symbols.risk: An oil re-spike re-pins CAD; a hot AU CPI lifts AUD.

symbols.why: Dovish-hike NZD vs a jobs/oil-firmed CAD; both catalysts spent.
symbols.risk: NZ Q2 CPI vs Canada oil/data set the break.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a yield-weak JPY - mild carry-long tilt, but intervention risk near 163 caps it.
symbols.risk: A no-warning MoF strike; a soft UK data wave.

symbols.why: Firm hawkish GBP (services 3.7%) vs a re-firmed USD - two firm legs cancel; no momentum until CPI/UK CPI.
symbols.risk: A hot US CPI presses it under 1.34; a hawkish UK CPI (~Jul 22) re-firms GBP.

symbols.why: Post-RBNZ dovish hike vs a re-firmed USD - the firm dollar leads, dovish NZD caps the bounce.
symbols.risk: NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) re-opens direction; a hot US CPI.

symbols.why: Two soft legs; neither CB has a near catalyst.
symbols.risk: The AU Q2 CPI late July sets direction.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a mixed AUD - mild long tilt, headline-hostage.
symbols.risk: De-escalation extends AUD; a hot AU CPI.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a delivered-but-dovish RBNZ - no vector post-event.
symbols.risk: NZ Q2 CPI; BoE Jul 30.

symbols.why: Re-firmed to ~101 by Friday's close as higher-for-longer reasserted and EUR (57.6% wt) sank to a 1-yr low; the SEP caps the downside and the tilt is now up - but Jul 14 CPI is the only trigger that resets direction.
symbols.risk: A soft/negative headline CPI misread dovish breaks 100.5; a hot core snaps it above 101.5.

symbols.why: JPY weakened (spot ~162.5) so the basket leaked lower - a mild short tilt, capped only by the MoF backstop-asymmetry near 163. (Rises when JPY strengthens - opposite ticket to XXX/JPY.)
symbols.risk: A break to 163+ triggers MoF and snaps the basket UP; a hot US CPI extends the drift lower.

symbols.why: Stretched at record territory (~25,800); the weekly oil gain is now a mild headwind vs the hawkish-ECB/geopolitics backdrop - a live two-way.
symbols.risk: A Hormuz re-escalation turns it SHORT; a clean ceasefire restores the melt-up.

symbols.why: Gold held ~$4,122 (-1.5% on the week) as a firmer USD and steadier yields capped it while the Hormuz haven bid underpinned - trade levels until the Fed path settles.
symbols.risk: A hot CPI re-arms the real-rate selloff; a Hormuz shock that keeps a haven bid revives the long.

symbols.why: Silver snapped back to ~$60.4 (from ~$57.7, ratio ~68) - the industrial-demand leg plus the haven bid drove a sharp recovery that outpaced gold; firmer tone but two-way into CPI.
symbols.risk: A high-beta flush lower on a hot-CPI real-rate spike; follows gold down if the haven leg fades.

catalysts.note: Mediators (Qatar/Pakistan/Oman/Turkey/Egypt/Saudi) working to reconvene; Trump says Iran 'reached out,' Iran's team 'distrustful' - first hard de-escalation vs live strikes drives oil, CAD, JPY, gold.
The 4.2%-regime check - oil base softens the headline but core seen firm (+0.3% m/m, ~2.9%); re-arms or kills the Sept hike (~45-50%). The single arbiter.
Hold; jobs beat but hollow internals + technical recession.
RBNZ says headline PEAKED at 3.9% - the print validates or breaks the 'higher hurdle' guidance.
Unemployment 4.5% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Hold; core-2.5% + oil-inflation is the Sept-hike signal.
Warsh round 2; hold + hawkish minimalism; Sept ~45-50% hangs on the Jul 14 CPI.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~162.5 and the MoF zone hot.
June-17 MoU's negotiating window ends; Hormuz administration the core dispute.
3 vessels hit, US strikes Jul 7-9 (~90 targets), MoU 'over'; every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold.

geopolitics: The kinetic story is live and the ceasefire is fragile. After Iran fired on three commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, US Central Command struck ~90 targets across Iran on Jul 7-9, and President Trump declared the June-17 Islamabad MoU 'over,' calling further talks 'a waste of time.' Yet by the weekend the diplomatic track was being restitched: technical negotiators (with VP Vance, Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff on the US side; Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Araghchi for Iran) had convened in Switzerland, mediators from Qatar, Pakistan, Oman, Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia were working to reconvene, and Trump said Iran had 'reached out.' Iran's team remains publicly 'distrustful,' and the Strait of Hormuz - its future administration - is the unresolved core, with traffic still well below pre-war levels. That standoff put a risk premium back into crude: WTI closed ~$71.2 but +3.5% on the week (Brent ~$76.5), even as the stacked Iranian-plus-Saudi glut capped the upside. Transmission by currency: the USD re-firmed (DXY back to ~101) as higher-for-longer reasserted into CPI - the war-trade beneficiary is the firm leg again; CAD drew a floor from its own jobs beat plus the weekly oil gain, holding USD/CAD off its highs; JPY weakened to ~162.5 as US yields firmed, with the MoF backstop the only remaining cap; EUR sank to a one-year low on the firm-USD tape even as oil-inflation lifts its own Sept-hike odds; gold held ~$4,122 on the haven bid while silver snapped back to ~$60.4 on the industrial-plus-haven combination; GER40 carries the oil gain as a mild headwind at record highs. The two-way risk is large and unchanged: a Doha breakthrough crushes what premium remains, while a genuine Hormuz-closure or tanker shock re-arms the whole complex overnight - which is why the oil-linked calls are sized to a whisper and the board carries no high-conviction pairs.
